description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a hip roof covered in dark brown tiles. The exterior walls are finished with a combination of light-colored brickwork and vertical accent columns, giving the structure a modern yet classic appearance. Large rectangular windows are symmetrically arranged on both floors, with a prominent bay window feature on one side. The main entryway is sheltered by a small porch with a gabled roof, supported by two columns and accessed by a short set of steps. A chimney protrudes from the roof, and there is a secondary side entrance visible. The house sits on a concrete foundation with a small front stoop and a paved area leading to the door.
description: A two-story house with a dark hip roof, light brick walls, large windows, and a covered front porch with columns.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with a porch, two stories of windows, and the hip roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long brick wall, secondary entrance, and the depth of the roof structure.
description_view_2: Rear angled view displaying the bay window, chimney, and additional windows on both floors.
description_view_3: Back view with a single tall window and the rear roof slope, minimal architectural detailing.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with bay window feature
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with gabled roof, two supporting columns, steps, and a single main door
